# 📓 Developer's Diary – AI Collaboration

---

\#\#\# Entry 1 – Creating sample data transactions

\*\*Artifact:\*\* ![][image1]

![][image2]

![][image3]

![][image4]

![][image5]

\*\*Context:\*\* Create a realistic transaction data with specific categories for analysis 

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Create a sample transaction data that contains date, category, amount, description. The date starts from 2026- 04-01 till 2026-04-30.  The different categories are, Entertainment,  Food, Transport, Refund, Clothes. There should be dollar signs infront of each amount. Some descriptions could be, Bowling tickets, Cinema Tickets, Coffee at Starbucks, Clothes purchased at Woolworth, Bus ticket, Refund for in-game purchases Mobile legends, McDonalds Big Mac meal, Snacks. Use real australian businesses as  description and use realistic amount. The data should reflect realistic expense of an average person in australia. You can add more descriptions using your own creativity but stick to these categories given. The refunds should be in negative value as this list is for expenditures and the only income is refunds."

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* Claude provided the data as per request with the categories, amount, date and description.

\*\*My Critique/Improvement:\*\* To improve the data I adjusted the signs of the expenses to positive and refunds to negative. Additionally, I also limited it to one expense per day to make it simple for analysis.

\*\*Result:\*\* I ended up with a clear data with the appropriate signs. It is better for analysis as per the sample data given.

\*\*Reflection:\*\* With the help of Claude, I was able to get a data that will give real life applicable advise since, it generated a data reflective of an average person which I would have difficulty to come up on my own. 

---

\#\#\# Entry 2 – Brainstorming Specific Finance Problems 

\*\*Artifact:\*\* ![][image6]![][image7]

\*\*Context:\*\* To brainstorm and identify problems that a csv analysis tool can solve 

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Help me brainstorm specific personal finance problems that could be solved  
with a CSV analysis tool. I'm interested in helping people with budgeting  
and spending awareness. What are common financial challenges that data  
analysis could address?  
\]"

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* Claude highlighted some key ideas such as behavioural patterns, Goal tracking, Spending awareness that could be used in the analysis tool.

\*\*Reflection:\*\* Claude helped me to get a direction in which I could develop the analysis tool. 

---

\#\#\# Entry 3 – Reviewing my pseudocode

\*\*Artifact:\*\* ![][image8]

![][image9]

![][image10]

![][image11]

![][image12]

![][image13]

![][image14]

\*\*Context:\*\* For claude to review my pseudocode for missing edges and improve business logic

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Review my pseudocode for analyzing spending data from a CSV. Are there any  
edge cases I'm missing? What business logic should I add to make this more  
valuable for personal finance insights?   
FUNCTION analyse\_transactions\_data(csv\_file):  
  // Step 1: Load and validate csv file   
  \- Read CSV file   
  \- check data for required columns (Categories, Date, Amount, Description)  
  \- handle missing value or invalid data

  // Step 2: Clean financial data  
  \- Remove dollar signs from amount column  
  \- Create separate column for refunds  
  \- convert to numeric value from string to compute calculations

  // Step 3: Calculate Business Metrics  
  \- Compute Total spending for the month and for weekly   
  \- Total transaction by category  
  \- Calculate average cost for the whole month and weekly  
  \- identify outliers, week where weekly exceeds average for the month

  // Step 4: Generate Insights  
  \- Identify maximum amount item spent for the weekly total   
  \- Percentage change in weekly spending as the month goes  
  \- Suggest saving opportunities 

  // Step 5: Format for presentation  
  \- Add explanatory text for insights  
  \- include currency formatting in output as it was removed earlier for computation  
  \- create business-friendly and easy to understand output

RETURN formatted\_analysis

"

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* Claude provided a detailed analysis and review of my pseudocode and how to improve it 

\*\*Reflection:\*\* One key information was about the ineffectiveness of the weekly average for a single month data, then I used the two 15 day period instead for better analysis of spending pattern at the start compared to at the end. 

---

\#\#\# Entry 4 – AI collaboration for loading and cleaning data

\*\*Artifact:\*\* ![][image15]

\*\*Context:\*\* clean the csv file data to be able to compute and perform calculations

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Based on my pseudocode create a function to load CSV transaction data with Date, Amount, Category,  
    Description columns. Handle dollar signs in Amount, missing values, and  
    data validation. Include clear business-focused error messages. Keep the code simple and with comments  
FUNCTION analyse\_transactions\_data(csv\_file):  
  // Step 1: Load and validate csv file   
  \- Read CSV file   
  \- check data for required columns (Categories, Date, Amount, Description)  
  \- handle missing value or invalid data

  // Step 2: Clean financial data  
  \- Remove dollar signs from amount column  
  \- Create separate column for refunds  
  \- convert to numeric value from string to compute calculations

  // Step 3: Calculate Business Metrics  
  \- Total transaction by category  
  \- Calculate average cost for each category   
  \- identify outliers  
  \- Calculate 15 day period totals and average to see when is spending more

  // Step 4: Generate Insights  
  \- Identify top items on which spent the most   
  \- Suggest whether spending more beginning or end of month  
  \- Suggest spending habbits to better manage budget  
  \- Suggest saving opportunities 

  // Step 5: Format for presentation  
  \- Add explanatory text for insights  
  \- include currency formatting in output as it was removed earlier for computation  
  \- create business-friendly and easy to understand output

RETURN formatted\_analysis  
""

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* The AI provided a code that was complicated at first but manage which was hard to understand.

\*\*My Critique/Improvement:\*\* To keep the code simple to understand and use frequent comments.

\*\*Result:\*\* The code is now easy to understand. 

\*\*Reflection:\*\* AI can at first generate complex code to get the desired result but is not ideal for learning. 

---

\#\#\# Entry 5 – Create function to analyse spending patterns and give insights

\*\*Artifact:\*\* ![][image16]

![][image17]  
![][image18]

\*\*Context:\*\* Create a function that analyses spending pattern and generate financial insight on how to save money. Using simple codes and comments.

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Create a function that analyses spending pattern and generate  actionable financial insight. It should calculate total spending, total spending per category, identify outliers, top expensive items, calculate 2 15 day period at the start and at the end in order to see which period spending is the most, calculate percentage of total spending expensive items are. Use simple language and use comments"

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* A function that calculates the highest expenditure, percentage of expenditure and gives insights to reduce highest expenses 

\*\*Reflection:\*\* It divides its codes into small chunks instead of writing in some long complicated lines. It is easier to spot mistakes this way and understand the code better. 

---

\#\#\# Entry 6 – Financial professional recommendation

\*\*Artifact:\*\* ![][image19]  
![][image20]

![][image21]

\*\*Context:\*\* Create professional financial recommendation

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Based on spending analysis data, create professional financial  
    recommendations. Include specific savings opportunities, spending  
    pattern observations, and actionable advice formatted for a  
    personal finance app user."

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* Provided a code that generates full financial report that summarises and give insights to the expenses 

\*\*Reflection:\*\* setting limits to the budget of the month allows the person to get warning messages that help him to know where and how to save money.   
---

\#\#\# Entry 7 – Financial Advice Chatbot

\*\*Artifact:\*\* ![][image22]

\*\*Context:\*\* Create a system using hands\_on\_ai to prompt chatbot to give professional financial insights

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Help me create a system prompt for a friendly, professional financial  
    advisor chatbot that can provide spending advice based on transaction  
    analysis. The personality should be encouraging but practical."

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* AI provided a chatbot function with a detailed system prompt defining the personality to give professional financial insights

\*\*Reflection:\*\* I realised that a finance chatbot like this could be really useful for people who are too embarrassed to ask a financial advisor basic money questions — it gives honest advice without any judgment. 

---

\#\#\# Entry 8 – \[Descriptive Title\]

\*\*Artifact:\*\* ![][image23]

![][image24]

![][image25]

![][image26]

\*\*Context:\*\* Set up RAG system to answer user question

\*\*My Prompt:\*\*"Help me set up a RAG system that can retrieve information from financial documents, budgeting guides, and transaction summaries to answer user questions about personal finance." 

\*\*My Critique/Improvement:\*\* The code kept failing with a 404 error because get\_embeddings requires a local Ollama server which doesn't run on Google Colab. I had to replace it with a simple keyword matching approach that scored chunks based on how many question words they contained. 

\*\*Result:\*\* The RAG system works correctly without needing any local server. It creates a finance knowledge document, splits it into chunks and finds the most relevant chunks to answer any finance question. 

\*\*Reflection:\*\* I learned that RAG is useful in business because instead of giving everyone the same generic advice, a company could feed it their own documents and policies so customers get answers specific to that business. 

---

\#\#\# Entry 8 – Building a Savings Goal Calculator Tool 

\*\*Artifact:\*\* ![][image27]

![][image28]

![][image29]

\*\*Context:\*\* I needed to create a savings calculator tool that tells users how long it will take to reach their savings goal. 

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Create a savings goal calculator function that takes current savings, monthly contribution, and target amount, then calculates time to reach goal. Format output for user friendly display."

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* AI provided a calculator function with input validation, months to years conversion and a formatted output display, registered as an agent tool. 

\*\*My Critique/Improvement:\*\* The agent.run\_agent() call kept hitting max iterations without giving an answer so I replaced it with a direct function call instead which is simpler and more reliable. 

\*\*Result:\*\*The calculator works correctly and displays a clean formatted result showing exactly how long it takes to reach any savings goal. 

\*\*Reflection:\*\*Sometimes the simplest solution is the best one — skipping the agent and calling the function directly gave a better result faster, which is a good lesson for business too. 

---

\#\#\# Entry 9 – Building the Gradio UI for the Finance Assistant 

\*\*Artifact:\*\*   
![][image30]

![][image31]

![][image32]

![][image33]

![][image34]

\*\*Context:\*\* I needed to create a user friendly Gradio interface that brings together all my existing functions into one application. 

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Help me design a Gradio interface that combines CSV upload, spending analysis, chat functionality, and custom tools in a user friendly layout suitable for a personal finance application." 

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* AI provided a complete Gradio interface with three tabs — spending analysis, finance chatbot and savings calculator — using gr.Blocks and gr.ChatInterface. 

\*\*My Critique/Improvement:\*\* The first version had duplicate cleaning code inside the UI instead of using my existing functions. I shared my actual code for each function one by one and asked AI to update the UI to call them directly instead of rewriting them. 

\*\*Result:\*\* The final UI calls my exact load\_and\_clean\_transaction\_data, analyze\_spending\_patterns, create\_finance\_chat\_personality and create\_savings\_calculator\_tool functions directly — no duplicate code anywhere.

\*\*Reflection:\*\* I learned that in business, a good UI is just a front door to the logic behind it — the real value is in the functions doing the work, and the interface just makes it easy for anyone to use without knowing how to code. 

---

\#\#\# Entry 10 – Creating Test Datasets 

\*\*Artifact:\*\*   
![][image35]

![][image36]  
\*\*Context:\*\* I needed realistic financial data to properly test all parts of my system.

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Create realistic test datasets for a finance assistant including:  
    1\. Normal spending data with various categories  
    2\. Edge cases: refunds (negative amounts), missing data, zero amounts  
    3\. Data quality issues: invalid formats, extreme values  
    4\. Business scenarios: high spending months, savings patterns  
    Include Australian business names and realistic amounts."

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* AI generated structured transaction datasets covering multiple financial situations.

\*\*Reflection:\*\*I learned how to simulate real-world financial data for testing.

---

\#\#\# Entry 11  – Data Loading Tests 

\*\*Artifact:\*\*   
![][image37]  
\*\*Context:\*\* I needed to ensure my CSV loading and cleaning function works correctly under different data conditions 

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Create assert statements to test my data loading function with:  
    \- Valid CSV data  
    \- CSV with dollar signs in amounts  
    \- Missing values and invalid data  
    \- Empty files and corrupted data  
    Verify that cleaning works correctly and errors are handled gracefully."

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* AI produced full structured test cases with assertions.

\*\*Reflection:\*\* I learned that data testing must focus on correctness, not fixed output sizes.

---

\#\#\# Entry 12 – Spending Analysis Tests 

\*\*Artifact:\*\*   
![][image38]

![][image39]  
\*\*Context:\*\* I needed to verify that financial calculations like totals, categories and refunds are correct 

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Create tests for spending analysis that verify:  
    \- Category totals are calculated correctly  
    \- Percentages add up to 100%  
    \- Refunds are handled appropriately  
    \- Edge cases like single transactions or empty categories  
    Use assert statements with known expected results."

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* AI created logic-based financial validation tests 

\*\*Reflection:\*\*I learned how to validate financial logic using controlled datasets 

---

\#\#\# Entry 13 – Business Insights Tests 

\*\*Artifact:\*\*  
![][image40]

\*\*Context:\*\* I needed to test whether my system generates correct financial recommendations 

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Create tests that verify business insights are appropriate:  
    \- High spending categories are identified correctly  
    \- Savings opportunities are realistic  
    \- Recommendations match spending patterns  
    \- Output format is user-friendly"

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\*AI suggested rule-based tests for insights and savings logic 

\*\*Reflection:\*\*I learned how AI helps design structured decision-making logic   
---

\#\#\# Entry 14 –  Integration Testing 

\*\*Artifact:\*\*   
![][image41]

![][image42]

\*\*Context:\*\* I wanted to ensure the Smart Finance Assistant works correctly across the full workflow from input data to final financial recommendations 

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Create an end-to-end test that:  
    1\. Loads sample CSV data  
    2\. Runs complete analysis pipeline  
    3\. Generates chat responses about the data  
    4\. Verifies RAG system retrieval  
    5\. Tests custom tool functionality  
    Ensure all components work together seamlessly."

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* AI produced a structured integration test covering all major system components including data processing, analysis, AI chat, retrieval system, and savings calculator tool 

\*\*My Critique/Improvement:\*\* I added assertion checks and a clear PASS/FAIL return system to ensure the test gives a reliable result 

\*\*Result:\*\* A complete integration test that verifies the full workflow of the finance assistant and confirms system stability 

\*\*Reflection:\*\*I learned how to validate a full system pipeline using assertions and structured testing instead of checking functions individually   
---

\#\#\# Entry 15 – Error Handling Testing

\*\*Artifact:\*\*   
![][image43]

![][image44]

![][image45]

\*\*Context:\*\* I wanted to ensure the Smart Finance Assistant does not crash and handles real-world bad data safely 

\*\*My Prompt:\*\* "Create tests that verify error handling for:  
    \- Invalid file uploads  
    \- Network connection issues  
    \- Malformed data  
    \- User input validation  
    Ensure error messages are user-friendly and helpful."

\*\*AI Response Summary:\*\* AI provided structured test cases for multiple failure scenarios with exception handling

\*\*Reflection:\*\* I learned that good software must handle failure cases properly, not just successful inputs, to be reliable in real-world use
